title: Tangy Tender Tunisian Chicken Wings Tagine
description: A tangy and savory Tunisian dish featuring chicken wings in a rich and cheesy tagine sauce, perfect for a midnight craving. This macrobiotic-friendly recipe uses nimono-simmer method for tender and flavorful wings.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken wings to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the marinade.
Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and arrange the chicken wings in a single layer.
Bake the chicken wings in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
While the chicken is baking, prepare the tagine sauce by mixing together the feta cheese and 1/4 cup of water in a blender or food processor.
After the chicken has baked for 30 minutes, brush the tagine sauce evenly over the chicken wings.
Return the chicken to the oven and bake for an additional 20 minutes, or until the sauce is caramelized and the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the chicken wings hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
